put.summary: Apply a Payment Transaction to an Invoice
put.description:
A payment transaction can only be applied to an invoice if:
- the transaction is a payment (rather than a write-off or a refund)
- the payment transaction is not already applied to the invoice
- the payment transaction and invoice are in the same project
- the amount to apply is not greater than the invoice outstanding balance
- the amount to apply is not greater than the unapplied amount of the payment transaction

put.description:
Performs a partial update of billing settings for a project. Only fields provided in the request body (non-null values) will be updated.

Updatable Fields:
- DefaultRateScheduleID: Default rate schedule for billing
- DefaultTermsID: Default invoice payment terms
- RuleSetID: Billing rules configuration
- LedesClientMatterID: LEDES client matter identifier (max 40 characters)
- InitialFunds: Initial trust/retainer funds amount (must be positive)
- FundsThreshold: Low balance threshold for trust account (must be positive)
- InvoiceTemplateID: Template for invoice generation
- TimeCodeOneRequired: Require first time code on time entries
- TimeCodeTwoRequired: Require second time code on time entries
- ExpenseCodeOneRequired: Require first expense code on expense entries
- ExpenseCodeTwoRequired: Require second expense code on expense entries
- FlatFeeCodeOneRequired: Require first code on flat fee entries
- FlatFeeCodeTwoRequired: Require second code on flat fee entries
- ListOnInvoicePersonID: Person to list on invoices
- ListOnInvoiceContactID: Contact to list on invoices

Validation:
- All ID fields (RateScheduleID, TermsID, TemplateID, PersonID, ContactID) are validated against the organization's data
- LedesClientMatterID cannot exceed 40 characters
- InitialFunds and FundsThreshold must be positive values
- User must have admin permission on the project

Returns 200 OK with updated settings on success, 400 Bad Request for validation errors, or 403 Forbidden if user lacks permission.

parameters.name: x-fv-userid
parameters.description: The User Id of the requesting user

parameters.name: x-fv-orgid
parameters.description: The Org Id the requesting user belongs to

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: The settings to update. Only non-null fields will be updated.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of documents. Org Admin access is required, unless providing a filter that indicates otherwise.

parameters.name: includeFullUserName
parameters.description: When true, includes the full name of the user who uploaded the document.

parameters.name: filterEndDateByTime
parameters.description: Set this to true if you want date filtering to include the time portion of endDate. If this param is false, the app will convert endDate to the server's time zone, then add 1 day to endDate, and set endDate's time to midnight in the server's time zone.

parameters.name: filterStartDateByTime
parameters.description: Set this to true if you want date filtering to include the time portion of startDate. If this param is false, startDate's time is set to midnight in the server's time zone.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: ISO 8601 UTC format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ e.g., 2026-03-14T02:30:00.123Z. You must convert your time to UTC time zone. The resultset will include documents with an upload date less than (but not equal to) endDate. See also: filterEndDateByTime

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: ISO 8601 UTC format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ e.g., 2026-03-14T02:30:00.123Z. You must convert your time to UTC time zone. The resultset will include documents with an upload date greater than or equal to startDate. See also: filterStartDateByTime.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return. This defaults to 50 with a soft-cap at 1000. Even if more are requested, only 1000 will be returned in a single response.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: requestedFields
parameters.description:
When retrieving a list of items from an endpoint, you can specify the top-level fields returned for each item in that list.

For example, `GET /contacts?requestedFields=firstname,lastname,age` would return the list of contacts with each contact having only their first name, last name, and age as their fields.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description:
Filters documents by filename (case-insensitive substring match).

Tag search: if the value begins with `#`, the parameter is interpreted as a tag (hashtag) name and results are filtered to documents that have a tag whose name matches exactly. The provided value is normalized before matching — whitespace is converted to dashes, characters that are not letters/digits/dashes are stripped, and the value is truncated to 75 characters. For example, `searchTerm=#urgent` returns documents tagged `#urgent`.

Important: `#` is reserved in URLs as the fragment delimiter and must be URL-encoded as `%23` when sent as a query parameter. For example: use `?searchTerm=%23urgent`, not `?searchTerm=#urgent`. Without encoding, most clients (browsers, curl without quoting, etc.) strip everything after the `#` before the request is sent, and the hashtag filter will silently not apply.

Updates document metadata. All fields are optional; only provided fields are applied.

**Notes:**
- When moving a document to a folder that already contains a file with the same name, the filename will be automatically adjusted to avoid a collision. Always verify the `Filename` in the returned document.
- File extensions provided in `Filename` field will not overwrite an existing extension; the document retains its original extension.
- Providing `Hashtags` replaces all existing tags on the document; omit the field entirely to leave tags unchanged.

post.description:
Adding a document revision is a multistep process. First, you must upload the revision document itself using ["Create Document URL for Upload"](./bb31213cefa02-create-document-url-for-upload) and then stream the file to the pre-signed URL. The response of ["Create Document URL for Upload"](./bb31213cefa02-create-document-url-for-upload) will include a document ID that you can use in this request as a body parameter, so that the revision document can be associated with the original document (Original Document’s Document ID will be the path parameter for this request).

**Constraints:**
- Requires `Full` access to the original document. A Guest is denied with `403` even for a document that has been shared with them.
- The revision document must be the same file type (filename extension) as the original document; a mismatch is rejected.
- The revision document must be pending (uploaded but not yet committed). A committed document returns `412`.
- On success the document keeps its `DocumentId`, but its `UploaderId`, `UploadDate`, and `Size` are replaced with the revision document's values.

parameters.name: documentId
parameters.description: The original Document ID for which the revision is going to be applied. Use prefix `@` to signify the use of a Partner ID.

parameters.name: x-fv-userid
parameters.description: The User Id of the requesting user

parameters.name: x-fv-orgid
parameters.description: The Org Id the requesting user belongs to

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: The identifier of the already-uploaded revision document to attach (the document ID returned by "Create Document URL for Upload"). Reference it by `Native` (numeric) document ID or by `Partner` ID.

200.description: Returns the updated document. It retains its original DocumentId but takes on the revision document's UploaderId, UploadDate, and Size.

400.description: Bad Request: the document ID path parameter is invalid (non-numeric, or a bare `@`), or the request body identifier is empty (neither a Native nor a Partner ID).

403.description: Forbidden: the caller lacks Full access to the original document (including a Guest, or a cross-org request), or the original document might not exist.

404.description: Not Found: the revision document does not exist (including a revision that was already claimed and therefore deleted), or the original document has been deleted.

409.description: Conflict: another revision is currently being applied to this document. Retry the request.

412.description: Precondition Failed: the revision document is not pending (it has already been committed).

422.description: Unprocessable Request: the original or the revision document is in a state that cannot be used as a revision (e.g. it is a contract document).

post.description: Sets an advisory lock on a document, reserving it for the calling user. The lock carries a 24-hour expiration. Re-locking a document you already hold succeeds and renews the expiration. A lock held by another user can be taken only if that lock has expired, or if the caller is an org admin. The lock is advisory and is not currently enforced by document mutation endpoints.

403.description: Forbidden: the caller is not a full org member, the document is in a protected folder the caller's team cannot access, or the document does not exist or has been deleted (its existence is not disclosed).

409.description: Conflict: the document is locked by another user.

post.description: Clears the advisory lock on a document. Unlocking a document that is not locked, or one you already hold, succeeds. A lock held by another user can be cleared only if that lock has expired, or if the caller is an org admin. The lock is advisory and is not currently enforced by document mutation endpoints.

post.summary: Bulk Assign Mailroom Items to a Project
post.description:
Moves one or more mailroom items to their respective target projects. Each assignment independently specifies a mailroom item and a project, so items can be routed to different projects in a single call. Each item's associated note and documents are reassigned to the target project and removed from the mailroom inbox.
Returns 204 No Content when all assignments succeed; 207 Multi-Status with per-item results when one or more fail.
The authenticated user must have mailroom access on the org and Full access on each target project.
Per-item status codes: 200 (success), 400 (invalid input), 403 (access denied or item not found), 404 (project not found).
Note: a per-item 403 is returned both when the caller lacks access to a mailroom item and when the item does not exist, to prevent leaking the existence of items belonging to other organizations.

parameters.name: x-fv-userid
parameters.description: The User Id of the requesting user

parameters.name: x-fv-orgid
parameters.description: The Org Id the requesting user belongs to

parameters.name: request

One or more assignment operations. Each entry independently specifies the mailroom item
            and the project it should be moved into, allowing items to be routed to different projects
            in a single call.

parameters.description: Body containing a list of Assignments, each with a MailroomItemId and a ProjectId.

204.description: No Content: all items assigned successfully.

207.description: Multi-Status: one or more items failed. Response contains per-item results (Id, Status, Error).

post.description:
Adds a new comment to the specified note. Side effects: creates a comment record and related audit activity.
**Body** is optional. **AuthorId** is optional: when omitted (or empty), the author is the authenticated API user (see request headers). When **AuthorId** is set, it identifies the user stored as the comment author (native ID or partner string per Identifier rules).
Optional: **Documents** (each item must include **DocumentId** when attaching files).
**Idempotency:** If **CommentId.Partner** is set and maps to an existing comment in the org, the API returns that comment and does not create a new one; **Body**, **AuthorId**, and **Documents** are not used on that path.
Identifiers use integer native IDs or partner strings (e.g. @external-123) per Filevine Identifier rules.

parameters.name: noteId
parameters.description: Note identifier. Integer (native ID, e.g. 12345) or partner ID prefixed with @ (e.g. @external-123).

parameters.name: x-fv-userid
parameters.description: The User Id of the requesting user

parameters.name: x-fv-orgid
parameters.description: The Org Id the requesting user belongs to

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Create payload. Use property names from CreateCommentRequest (Body, CommentId, AuthorId, Documents); extra properties are ignored on deserialize. A JSON body is required (empty object is allowed). For idempotent lookup: CommentId.Partner may be sufficient when it resolves to an existing comment.

200.description: Success. Returns the created comment with server-assigned IDs, or the existing comment when idempotent on CommentId.Partner.

400.description: Bad Request. Missing body, invalid noteId or comment identifier format, or malformed body.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid authentication token.

403.description: Forbidden. User lacks permission to add comments on this note.

404.description: Not Found. The note path identifier cannot be resolved, the note is missing, or (when CommentId.Partner resolves to an existing comment) that comment is not on the specified note. An unregistered CommentId.Partner does not return 404; the API creates a new comment.

post.description:
Creates a new note in the specified project. The request body is required.
Required: ProjectId (Identifier with Native or Partner).
Optional: body, subject, authorId (defaults to authenticated user), documents, NoteId.partner (for idempotency), source, destination, createdAt, typeTag, participantUserIDs, participantContactPersonIDs, phoneCallStartTime, phoneCallEndTime, isPinnedToProject, isPinnedToFeed.
Tags and assigneeId are not applied on create; use PATCH /v2/Notes/{noteId} after creation if needed.
Omitting ProjectId returns 400 Bad Request.
If NoteId.Partner is provided and a note with that partner ID exists, returns the existing note (idempotency).

parameters.name: x-fv-userid
parameters.description: The User Id of the requesting user

parameters.name: x-fv-orgid
parameters.description: The Org Id the requesting user belongs to

parameters.name: request

ProjectId.description: Project identifier. Required. Use Native (integer) or Partner (external ID).

NoteId.description: Client idempotency key: set Partner to reuse an existing note when that partner ID is already mapped. Native is ignored on create.

Body.description: Note body. Optional; defaults to empty.

AuthorId.description: Author identifier. Optional; defaults to the authenticated user.

Source.description: Email source address. Optional. Only values that pass email validation are forwarded; otherwise treated as null.

Destination.description: Email destination address. Optional. Only values that pass email validation are forwarded; otherwise treated as null.

CreatedAt.description:
Creation timestamp. Optional; must not be in the future. Omit for server time.
            Format: ISO 8601 date-time (e.g. 2025-02-20T14:00:00Z).

TypeTag.description: When equal to "phcall" (case-insensitive), the note is created as a phone call.

ParticipantUserIDs.description: User IDs of note participants. Optional. Used when TypeTag is "phcall" to record call participants.

ParticipantContactPersonIDs.description: Contact person IDs of note participants. Optional. Used when TypeTag is "phcall" to record external call participants.

PhoneCallStartTime.description:
Phone call start time. Optional. Used when TypeTag is "phcall".
            Format: ISO 8601 date-time (e.g. 2025-02-20T14:00:00Z).

PhoneCallEndTime.description:
Phone call end time. Optional. Used when TypeTag is "phcall".
            Format: ISO 8601 date-time (e.g. 2025-02-20T14:00:00Z).

IsPinnedToProject.description: Pin to project. Optional; omit or null defaults to false.

IsPinnedToFeed.description: Pin to feed. Optional; omit or null defaults to false.

post.description:
Creates a new calendar appointment for the specified project. Use for scheduling. Side effects: persists the event and audit trail.
Required body fields: ProjectId (must identify the same project as the route `{projectId}`), Title, StartUtc, EndUtc, Attendees (minimum 1). Omitting any required field or sending an invalid body returns 400 Bad Request.
EventUniqueId is optional on create and ignored; the server generates EventUniqueId. Optional: CalendarEventType (defaults to Appointment when omitted; the route is appointment-only), Location, Notes, AllDay, AppointmentId.Partner (idempotency), TimeTrackingDetails, and other CalendarEvent fields not listed as required.
Dates: StartUtc and EndUtc must be ISO 8601 date-time in UTC (e.g. 2025-02-20T14:00:00Z).
Idempotency: Use AppointmentId.Partner when retrying the same logical create; EventUniqueId is not used for idempotency.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: Project identifier. Integer (native ID, e.g. 12345) or partner ID prefixed with @ (e.g. @external-123).

parameters.name: x-fv-userid
parameters.description: The User Id of the requesting user

parameters.name: x-fv-orgid
parameters.description: The Org Id the requesting user belongs to

parameters.name: request

EventUniqueId.description:
Optional. Stable unique identifier for the event (e.g. format: {Guid}@calendar.filevine.com).
            On create: ignored; server generates a new EventUniqueId automatically.
            Use AppointmentId.Partner for create idempotency, not EventUniqueId.

CalendarEventType.description:
Optional on create for project appointments (`POST /v2/Projects/{projectId}/Appointments`).
            When omitted or empty, the server creates an event of type `Appointment`.

AllDay.description: Specifies the event is an all-day event.

StartUtc.description: Event start time in UTC. Format: ISO 8601 date-time (e.g. 2025-02-20T14:00:00Z).

EndUtc.description: Event end time in UTC. Format: ISO 8601 date-time (e.g. 2025-02-20T15:00:00Z).

StartEditable.description:
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the start time of the calendar event can be edited.
            true if the start time of the calendar event can be edited; otherwise, false.

DurationEditable.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the duration of the calendar event is editable.

parameters.description: Calendar event payload. Required: ProjectId, Title, StartUtc, EndUtc, Attendees (min 1). Optional: EventUniqueId (ignored on create), CalendarEventType (defaults to Appointment), Location, Notes, AllDay, AppointmentId.Partner (idempotency), TimeTrackingDetails.

200.description: OK. Returns the created appointment with server-assigned AppointmentId and EventUniqueId.

400.description: Bad Request. Validation failed (missing required fields, invalid body, or business rules).

403.description: Forbidden. Missing or invalid authentication, or user lacks Calendars.Create / project access.

404.description: Not Found. Project does not exist or user lacks access.

post.description:
Every project has a [project email address](https://support.filevine.com/hc/en-us/articles/360004139292) where users can forward emails to store in Filevine. This call bypasses the [project email address](https://support.filevine.com/hc/en-us/articles/360004139292) and instantly posts a new email item to the project's activity feed.
Omitting the request body, omitting From, or omitting From.Address returns 400 Bad Request with a JSON error body. Invalid projectId format, missing auth, no access, or unknown project follow the usual 400/403/404 behavior for this API.

InitialFunds.description: Starting Project Funds amount when a project is created

DocID.description: The native document ID this scan result belongs to.

ThreatDetected.description: True when the scan recorded a threat. Computed from `Message` and `VirusName`: both must be non-null for this to be true. This is not a separately persisted verdict — it is derived from the scan-message fields on read.

Message.description: Engine-reported scan message. Null when the scan reported no findings.

VirusName.description: Engine-reported virus name when a threat was found. Null when the scan reported no findings.

DocKey.description: The `DocKey` identifying the specific revision/bytes that were scanned. Lets a single scan result be tied to one revision of the document.

Starting Project Funds amount when a project is created

InProgressBalance.description: Sum of the charged amount for all billing items that are on invoices that have not been finalized

ProjectFundsBalance.description: (Total Project Funds Deposits) - (Total Project Funds Debits)

BillableTotal.description:
The billable total after applying any invoice-level discounts.
            When no discounts are applied, this equals Total.

Optional. Stable unique identifier for the event (e.g. format: {Guid}@calendar.filevine.com).
            On create: ignored; server generates a new EventUniqueId automatically.
            Use AppointmentId.Partner for create idempotency, not EventUniqueId.

Optional on create for project appointments (`POST /v2/Projects/{projectId}/Appointments`).
            When omitted or empty, the server creates an event of type `Appointment`.

AllDay.description: Specifies the event is an all-day event.

StartUtc.description: Event start time in UTC. Format: ISO 8601 date-time (e.g. 2025-02-20T14:00:00Z).

EndUtc.description: Event end time in UTC. Format: ISO 8601 date-time (e.g. 2025-02-20T15:00:00Z).

StartEditable.description:
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the start time of the calendar event can be edited.
            true if the start time of the calendar event can be edited; otherwise, false.

DurationEditable.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the duration of the calendar event is editable.

TimeCode1.description: First time code for time tracking for the event.

TimeCode2.description: Second time code for time tracking for the event.

IsBillable.description: Indicates if the tracked time is billable to the client.

IsChargeable.description: Indicates if the tracked time is chargeable to the client.

IsDraft.description: Indicates whether this event is a draft.

Tags.description: Tags applied to this comment (from note tag rows scoped to the comment).

CommentId.description: Optional. Identifier for idempotent create. When CommentId.Partner matches an existing comment, returns that comment instead of creating a new one.

AuthorId.description: Optional. When omitted or empty, the authenticated API user is the author. When set, identifies the user stored as the comment author (native or partner Identifier).

Documents.description: Optional list of documents to attach. Each item must include DocumentId.
